search_queries_used: site:rcog.org.uk GTG 41 chronic pelvic pain history examination investigations
site:nice.org.uk ng126 pelvic pain pregnancy bleeding symptoms assessment ultrasound
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic ectopic pregnancy, miscarriage, infection, torsion or endometriosis conclusion
automatic pregnancy test, ultrasound, treatment or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: The workflow is broad and the cause remains clinician-assessed.
No investigation, procedure, treatment, medicine, dose or referral is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: RCOG evidence covers women with chronic pelvic pain; NICE evidence covers possible early-pregnancy complications. Acute non-pregnancy, pediatric, male and postoperative pelvic pain remain incompletely covered.
setting_applicability: Emergency pelvic-pain assessment with gynecology or early-pregnancy pathways when applicable.
UAE_applicability: Clinical domains transfer, but UAE pregnancy, gynecology, consent, imaging, laboratory, safeguarding and referral pathways govern.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG126 (2026) and the older RCOG GTG41 exact sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14; RCOG recency is a material limitation.
superseded_check: NICE NG126 is current; no newer RCOG chronic pelvic-pain guideline was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact RCOG chronic pelvic-pain history and examination section supports onset, duration, severity, impact, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, patient concerns, risk context and manual focused examination in women with chronic pain; it does not cover all acute ED pelvic pain.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies pain pattern, menstrual, bladder, bowel, movement, posture, function and medication history plus abdominal and pelvic examination for focal tenderness or structural findings.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: When pregnancy is possible or known, the exact NICE section supports recording vital data, time-critical symptoms and clinician-decided escalation without asserting ectopic pregnancy or miscarriage.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists pain, amenorrhoea, bleeding, gastrointestinal symptoms, dizziness or syncope, shoulder-tip pain, urinary symptoms, tenderness, haemodynamic signs, pregnancy testing, and escalation criteria.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: When early pregnancy applies, the exact ultrasound section supports recording imaging or test results already performed or reviewed, without recommending or interpreting them.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines clinician-performed transvaginal or selected transabdominal ultrasound and cautions against overinterpreting one early scan or LMP alone.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact initial-assessment section supports a clinician-entered plan, completed discussion and arranged follow-up only when documented; it does not generate management.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines chronic pelvic pain as a symptom rather than a diagnosis and frames initial assessment around the woman’s concerns, function, and multifactorial context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Investigation recommendations were reviewed but not mapped to automatic tests.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-selected STI sampling when PID is suspected and transvaginal ultrasound for adnexal masses and selected chronic pelvic-pain pathology.

unresolved_source_gaps: Acute non-pregnancy causes, male pelvic pain, detailed menstrual/sexual/urinary/bowel history, examination appropriateness, tests, diagnosis, treatment and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
